history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, indicating missing Jakarta Mail dependency and incomplete javax->jakarta source migration.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x migrates to Jakarta EE namespaces, removing javax.mail. The upgrade recipe did not add the jakarta.mail dependency or fully migrate the source references. Adding com.sun.mail:jakarta.mail via pom_patch ensures the classpath is satisfied, and explicitly including JavaxMigrationToJakarta forces the remaining javax.mail references to jakarta.mail.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' after Spring Boot 3 upgrade, indicating missing jakarta.mail dependency and un-migrated javax.mail source references.
history.qwen_rationale: The Spring Boot 3.x migration shifts from javax.* to jakarta.* namespaces. The previous attempt added the dependency but kept the migration recipe in the same step, likely causing a classpath resolution issue during compilation. Splitting the dependency injection, Spring Boot upgrade, and Jakarta namespace migration into distinct steps ensures the classpath is ready before source transformation, and the migration runs cleanly before compilation.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed during Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because source code still referenced javax.mail.MessagingException after the classpath was updated to Jakarta EE namespaces.
history.qwen_rationale: By combining UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 and JavaxMigrationToJakarta into a single step, OpenRewrite will apply both the framework upgrade and the namespace migration before running mvn compile, ensuring jakarta.mail imports are present and resolvable on the updated classpath.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' because the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade removes javax.mail dependencies before the source code is migrated to jakarta.mail namespaces.
history.qwen_rationale: Splitting the Jakarta namespace migration into a dedicated step before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade ensures all javax.mail imports and usages are rewritten to jakarta.mail while the classpath is still valid. Adding the jakarta.mail dependency via POM_PATCH beforehand guarantees the migration recipe can resolve the target types, preventing the compile error.

history.qwen_observation: The explicit JavaxMigrationToJakarta step ran before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, changing imports to jakarta.* while the classpath still contained Spring Boot 2.7 (javax.*), causing compilation mismatches and missing jakarta dependencies.
history.qwen_rationale: Remove the premature javax-to-jakarta migration and the standalone mail dependency step. Rely on UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 to handle the Spring Boot version bump, dependency updates, and javax-to-jakarta namespace migration atomically under JDK 17, which aligns with the framework's compatibility requirements.
